fast.description: Quick search, exploration, and read-only work.

medium.description: Default implementation, refactoring, and test work.

heavy.description: Deep analysis, debugging, and architecture work.

normal.description: Balanced default routing by task complexity.

ponytail.description: Prefer the shortest correct path, deletion, stdlib, and low ceremony.

deep.description: Use deep analysis for multi-step debugging and architecture work.

rules: Read-only exploration should favor the fast tier.
Implementation should default to medium unless the active mode says otherwise.
Architecture, security, and multi-system debugging should escalate to heavy.
Keep prompts concise and prefer the smallest correct change.
If native Claude subagents are unavailable, follow the same tier contract locally.
